top_signals.title: US says it hit 140 Iranian military targets as Tehran closes Strait of Hormuz
top_signals.summary: U.S. forces struck approximately 140 Iranian military targets on Saturday, bringing the total number of sites hit during three nights of operations to more than 300, after Washington accused Tehran of attacking a commercial vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. U.S. Central Command said the latest strikes targeted Iranian missile and drone sites, naval capabilities, ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Torvald Klaveness 1986–1996: Building the next big idea
top_signals.summary: By the mid-1980s, Klaveness had spent four decades finding new ways to move cargo. From fertilizer, cement, alumina, bauxite and grain, Klaveness had repeatedly challenged conventional thinking by designing transportation solutions around cargo flows rather than around vessels. New chapter, new ideas As shipping became increasingly competitive, Klaveness continued searching for ways to improve efficiency. ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Argentine wheat farmers slow sales as forward prices fall
top_signals.summary: Argentine farmers have sold only 2 million metric tons of their new wheat crop, marking one of the weakest starts in a decade, the Rosario grains exchange reported Friday. The slowdown comes as planting progresses rapidly but forward prices decline. Farmers have planted 82% of the 2026/27 wheat area projected by the government, with fieldwork ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: China marks 22nd National Maritime Day as marine economy tops 11 trillion yuan
top_signals.summary: China celebrated its 22nd National Maritime Day on Saturday, which also marks the country’s observance of World Maritime Day. This year’s theme of National Maritime Day highlights how digital intelligence is reshaping the future of navigation. The main event was held in Beijing for the first time in a hybrid format combining online and offline ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Is the AI Bubble About to Burst?
top_signals.summary: Artificial Intelligence has grown in popularity over the past five years, from something consumers only knew from sci-fi movies to a widely used technology. Many companies are now using AI in some form, and it has become part of everyday life for many consumers, with AI services integrated into internet search engines, phone applications, and other daily interactions. However, as AI is deployed at a faster pace, it is becoming clear that the technology cannot be used as extensively as tech companies had hoped, while companies are realising the…
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Hormuz traffic drops as ships go dark after latest attacks
top_signals.summary: Daily transits dropped to 20 vessels across both directions of the Strait on 9 July, following the latest vessel attacks in the region. This supports the observation that commercial routing decisions often lag behind major security developments, as vessels already in or near transit cannot simply stop immediately. We also observed renewed AIS spoofing activity ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
